45.text: This Law is made by the Ruler.

48.text: This Law applies in the jurisdiction of the DIFC.

50.text: This Law applies to all DIFC trusts, other than trusts created under the Investment Trust Law (DIFC Law No. 5 of 2006), and to foreign trusts in respect of all acts, omission or transaction occurring ...

238.text: 37. Validity and invalidity of a trust

239.text: (1) Subject to Articles 37(2), (3) and (4), a trust shall be valid and enforceable in accordance with its terms.

240.text: (2) A trust shall be invalid to the extent that:

241.text:
(a) it purports to do anything which is contrary to DIFC law;   
(b) it is created for a purpose other than a charitable purpose in relation to which there is no beneficiary, unless it complies with A...

242.text: (3) Where a trust is created for two (2) or more purposes of which some are lawful and others are unlawful:

243.text: (a) if those purposes cannot be separated the trust shall be invalid; or (b) where those purposes can be separated the Court may declare that the trust is valid as to the purposes which are lawful.

253.text: (5) A charitable trust may be enforced by the settlor of the trust, if the settlor is still living, maintaining a proceeding to enforce such trust or by the Court on the application of any of the sett...

(b) the purpose is not contrary to public policy in the DIFC or unlawful under the laws of the DIFC; and  ...

258.text: (3) It shall be the duty of an enforcer to enforce the trust in relation to its non-charitable purposes.

259.text: (4) The appointment of a person as enforcer of a trust in relation to its non-charitable purposes shall not have effect if he is also a trustee of the trust or has a conflict of interest.

260.text: (5) The terms of the trust may provide, or the Court may order, that some or all of the costs incurred by the enforcer in performing his duties shall be reimbursed to him out of the trust fund.

261.text: (6) Except as permitted by this Law or expressly provided by the terms of the trust, or with the approval of the Court an enforcer shall not:

263.text: (7) Subject to Article 39(8), an enforcer may resign his office by notice in writing delivered to the trustee. Such resignation shall take effect upon delivery of notice.

264.text: (8) A resignation given in order to facilitate a breach of trust shall be of no effect.

265.text: (9) An enforcer shall cease to be an enforcer of the trust in relation to its non-charitable purposes immediately upon:

266.text:
(a) the enforcer’s removal from office by the Court;   
(b) the enforcer’s resignation becoming effective;

(b) the provisions in the trust instrument are not effective to secure the appointment of a new enforcer; or...

274.text: 40. Variation and revocation of a trust

275.text: (1) A trust may expressly provide that:

276.text:
(a) its terms are capable of variation; or   
(b) the trust itself or a power exercisable under the trust is revocable either in whole or in part.

277.text: (2) Unless the terms of a trust expressly provide that the trust is revocable, it shall be irrevocable.

278.text: (3) Where a trust provides that the terms of the trust may be varied, such power to vary shall be without prejudice to the power vested in the Court by this Law for the variation of the terms of the t...

279.text: (4) No variation of the terms of the trust or revocation of a trust or a power exercisable under a trust shall prejudice anything lawfully done by a trustee in relation to a trust prior to his receivi...

280.text: (5) Subject to the terms of the trust, where a trust is revoked, either in whole or in part, the trustee shall hold the trust property affected by the revocation for the settlor absolutely or if the s...

281.text: (6) Where a power to revoke, a general power of appointment or the present beneficial interest in respect of all or part of the trust property is reserved or granted to a person, the trust instrument ...

284.text: (7) For the purposes of this Article, the “settlor” is the particular person who provided the property which is the subject of revocation.

285.text: (8) The Court may vary the terms of a trust:

286.text: (a) even if unambiguous, to conform the terms to the settlor’s intention if it is provided by clear and convincing evidence that both the settlor’s intent and the terms of the trust were affected by a...

287.text: (b) if, because of circumstances not anticipated by the settlor, modification will further the purpose of the trust; or

288.text: (c) if continuation of the trust on its existing terms would be impractical or wasteful or impair the administration of the trust,

289.text: with effect from either the date the order for variation of the trust was made or such earlier date as to the Court seems fit.

338.text: (1) The terms of a trust may make the interest of the beneficiary liable to termination.

340.text: (a) a restriction on alienation or disposal; or (b) a diminution or termination in the event of the beneficiary becoming insolvent or any of his property becoming liable to sequestration for the benef...

341.text: (3) A trust under which the interest of a beneficiary is subject to restriction, diminution or termination under Article 49(2) is a protective trust.

432.text: PART 8: DUTIES AND POWERS OF TRUSTEES

435.text: (1) Upon acceptance of a trusteeship, the trustee shall in the execution of his duties and in the exercise of his powers and discretions:

436.text:
(a) act with due diligence as would a prudent person to the best of his ability and skill; and   
(b) observe the utmost good faith,

437.text: in accordance with the terms and purposes of the trust and this Law.

438.text: (2) A trustee shall administer the trust solely in the interest of the beneficiaries or in furtherance or support of the purposes of the trust.

444.text: (5) A trustee shall keep trust property separate from his personal property and separately identifiable from any other property of which he is a trustee.

445.text: (6) A trustee of an express trust shall:

446.text: (a) take reasonable steps to identify the ultimate beneficial owners of any party to the trust which is a body corporate, including the settlor, enforcer, protector, beneficiaries and any other person...

474.text: (2) A trustee shall exercise his powers only in the interest of the beneficiaries and in furtherance and support of the purposes of the trust and in accordance with the terms of the trust.

476.text: Without limiting the generality of Article 67, and subject to the terms of the trust, a trustee may:

479.text: (a) collect trust property and accept or reject additions to the trust property from a settlor or any other person;

481.text: (c) acquire or sell property, for cash or on credit, at public or private sale;

482.text: (d) exchange, partition, or otherwise change the character of trust property;

483.text: (e) deposit trust money in an account in a regulated financial services institution;

484.text: (f) borrow money, with or without security, and mortgage or pledge trust property for a period within or extending beyond the duration of the trust;

485.text: (g) where the terms of a trust so permit, exercise a discretion in relation to the manner in which and to whom trust property is distributed;

486.text: (h) with respect to an interest in a partnership, limited liability company, business trust, corporation, or other form of business or enterprise, continue the business or other enterprise and take an...

495.text: (o) pay or contest any claim, settle a claim by or against the trust, and release, in whole or in part, a claim belonging to the trust;

496.text: (p) pay taxes, assessments, remuneration of the trustee, protector or enforcer and of employees and agents of the trust, and other expenses incurred in the administration of the trust;

497.text: (q) exercise elections with respect to any taxes;

(ii) if the trustee does not know of a guardian, or custo...

506.text: (y) prosecute or defend an action, claim, or judicial proceeding in any jurisdiction to protect trust property and the trustee in the performance of the trustee’s duties;

507.text: (z) sign and deliver contracts and other instruments that are useful to achieve or facilitate the exercise of the trustee’s powers; and

508.text: (aa) on termination of the trust, exercise the powers appropriate to wind up the administration of the trust and distribute the trust property to the persons entitled to it.

521.text: (1) A trustee shall not delegate his powers unless permitted to do so by this Law or by the terms of the trust.

526.text: (5) For the purposes of this Article an “agent” may include investment managers, accountants, lawyers, bankers, brokers, custodians, investment advisers, nominees, property agents, solicitors and othe...

527.text: (6) A trustee may authorise a person referred to in Article 70(5) to retain any commission or other payment usually payable in relation to any transaction.

543.text: PART 9: LIABILITY OF TRUSTEES AND RIGHTS OF PERSONS DEALING WITH A TRUSTEE

545.text: (1) Subject to this Law and to the terms of the trust, a trustee shall be liable for a breach of trust committed by the trustee or in which the trustee has concurred.

546.text: (2) A trustee who is liable for a breach of trust shall be liable for:

547.text:
(a) the loss or depreciation in value of the trust property resulting from such breach; and   
(b) the profit, if any, which would have accrued to the trust property if there had been no such breach.

548.text: (3) Where there are two (2) or more breaches of trust, a trustee shall not set off a gain from one (1) breach of trust against the loss resulting from another breach of trust.

549.text: A trustee shall not be liable for a breach of trust committed prior to his appointment, if such breach of trust was committed by some other person.

550.text: (5) A trustee shall not be liable for a breach of trust committed by a co-trustee unless:

551.text:
(a) he becomes aware or ought to have become aware of the commission of such breach or of the intention of his co-trustee to commit a breach of trust; and   
(b) he actively conceals such breach or su...

553.text: (a) relieve a trustee of liability to him for a breach of trust; or (b) indemnify a trustee against liability for a breach of trust.

554.text: (7) Article 73(6) shall not apply unless the beneficiary:

555.text:
(a) has legal capacity;   
(b) has full knowledge of all material facts; and   
(c) is not improperly induced by the trustee to take action under Article 73(6).   
(8) Where two (2) or more trustees a...

557.text: To remedy a breach of trust that has occurred or may occur, the Court may:

561.text: 75. Damages in absence of a breach

562.text: (1) Except as expressly provided in the terms of the trust, a trustee is accountable to the trust for any profit made by the trustee arising from the administration of the trust, even absent a breach ...

564.text: In a judicial proceeding involving the administration of a trust, the Court, as justice and equity may require, may award costs and expenses, including reasonable lawyers’ fees, to any party, to be pa...

566.text: (1) A person may not commence a proceeding against a trustee for breach of trust more than three (3) years after the date such person or a representative of such person receives a report from the trus...

567.text: (2) A report adequately discloses the existence of a potential claim for breach of trust if it provides sufficient information so that such person or representative knows of the potential claim or sho...

568.text: (3) If Article 77(1) does not apply, a judicial proceeding by such person against a trustee for breach of trust shall be commenced within seven (7) years of the first occurrence of:

570.text: (4) No period of limitation shall apply to an action brought against a trustee:

573.text: (a) in respect of any fraud to which the trustee was a party or to which the trustee was privy; or

574.text: (b) to recover from the trustee trust property:

576.text: (5) This Article applies also to proceedings brought against an enforcer or a protector.

578.text: Any person who acts in reasonable reliance on the terms of the trust as expressed in the trust instrument is not liable for a breach of trust to the extent the breach resulted from the reliance.

580.text: (1) A term of a trust relieving a trustee of liability for breach of trust is unenforceable to the extent that it:

581.text:
(a) relieves the trustee of liability for breach of trust committed in bad faith or with reckless indifference to the purposes of the trust or the interests of the beneficiaries; or   
(b) was inserte...

582.text: (2) Any insurance purchased and maintained by the trust in respect of a trustee must not include insurance in respect of any liability the trustee may incur:

585.text: (1) Subject to Article 73(10), a trustee is not liable to a beneficiary for breach of trust if the beneficiary consented to the conduct constituting the breach, released the trustee from liability for...

586.text:
(a) the consent, release, or ratification of the beneficiary was induced by improper conduct of the trustee; or   
(b) at the time of the consent, release, or ratification, the beneficiary did not kno...

587.text: (2) Where a trustee commits a breach of trust at the instigation or at the request or with the consent of a beneficiary, the Court may by order impound all or part of the interest of the beneficiary b...

590.text: 81. Limitation on personal liability of trustee

591.text: (1) Except as otherwise provided in the contract, a trustee is not personally liable on a contract properly entered into in the trustee’s fiduciary capacity in the course of administering the trust, i...

592.text: 82. Protection of persons dealing with trustees

593.text: (1) A person other than a beneficiary who in good faith assists a trustee, or who in good faith and for value deals with a trustee, without knowledge that the trustee is exceeding or improperly exerci...

594.text: (2) A person other than a beneficiary who in good faith deals with a trustee is not required to inquire into the extent of the trustee’s powers or the propriety of their exercise.

595.text: (3) A person who in good faith delivers assets to a trustee need not ensure their proper application.

596.text: (4) A person other than a beneficiary who in good faith assists a former trustee or who in good faith and for value deals with a former trustee, without knowledge that the trusteeship has terminated i...

597.text: (5) Comparable protective provisions of other DIFC laws relating to commercial transactions or transfer of securities by fiduciaries prevail over the protection provided by this Article.

598.text: 83. Trustees of more than one trust

599.text: A trustee acting as such for more than one (1) trust shall not, in the absence of fraud or bad faith, be affected by notice of matter in relation to a particular trust if the trustee has obtained noti...

601.text: PART 10: RESERVED AND RESTRICTED POWERS AND INTERESTS

603.text: (1) Without prejudice to the generality of Article 40, the reservation by the settlor to himself or grant to any other person (in this Law called the protector) in the trust instrument of a DIFC trust...

605.text: (2) The powers referred to in Article 84(1) are:

606.text:
(a) in the case of a reservation to the settlor or other donor of trust property, power to revoke the trusts in whole or in part;   
(b) power to vary or amend the terms of the trust instrument or any...

607.text: (c) a general, intermediate or special power to advance, appoint, pay, apply, distribute or transfer trust property (whether income or capital or both) or to give directions for the making of any such...

608.text: (d) power to act as, or give binding directions as to the appointment or removal of, a director or an officer of any company wholly or partly owned by the trust or to direct the trustee as to the mann...

609.text: (e) power to give binding directions in connection with the purchase, retention, holding, sale or other commercial or investment dealings with trust property or any investment or reinvestment thereof ...

610.text: power to give binding directions with regard to investment decisions, including the appointment of investment advisers or an investment committee whose decisions are to be implemented by the trustees;...

611.text: (g) power to give binding directions that the trustee shall not be under any duty nor be bound to interfere in the business of any company to which any such direction applies in which the trust is int...

614.text: (k) power to restrict the exercise of any powers, discretions or functions of a trustee by requiring that they shall only be exercisable with the consent, or at the direction of, any person specified ...

616.text: (a) has acted, or refrained from acting, in compliance with, or as a result of, a valid exercise of any of the powers set out in Article 84(2), shall not, by reason only of such compliance, commit a b...

617.text: (4) The trustee’s right to approach the Court under Article 20(1) includes the right to apply to the Court as to whether or not a direction pursuant to any of Articles 84(2)(d) to 84(2)(g) inclusive s...

618.text: (5) Whenever the terms of a DIFC trust provide that a trustee shall follow the direction of the protector with respect to decisions of the trustee, then, except to the extent that the trust provides o...

620.text: and absent evidence to the contrary, the actions of the trustee pertaining to matters within the scope of the protector’s authority (such as confirming that the protector’s directions have been carrie...

621.text: (6) No person other than a person in whom trust property or an interest in trust property is vested and who is formally appointed as a trustee, shall be or become a trustee by reason only of the reser...

622.text: The terms of a DIFC trust may provide that the reservation or grant of any of the powers set out in Article 84(2) shall not impose a fiduciary duty on the holder of such powers.

623.text: (8) In the absence of any contrary provision of the terms of a DIFC trust:

624.text: (a) in the case of the reservation by a settlor, or the grant to another person, of any of the powers specified in Article 84(2), where so long as the holder of the power is not the sole trustee, such...

625.text: (b) in any other case, such powers shall be fiduciary.

629.text: (1) If the settlor becomes incapacitated then the rights or powers (if any) reserved to him shall during his incapacitation be exercisable by the protector (if appointed) or by any other person design...

630.text: (2) If the protector becomes incapacitated then the rights or powers (if any) reserved to him shall during his incapacitation be exercisable by the person designated by the trust instrument or appoint...

631.text: (3) For the purposes of this Article:

632.text: (a) a person shall be deemed to be incapacitated if declared to be of unsound mind by a court of competent jurisdiction or if declared by two (2) qualified examining physicians to be of unsound mind o...

633.text: (b) a corporation shall be deemed to be incapacitated if it has been dissolved, or if it is under administration or a petition for its winding up has been presented in any Court and has not been final...

634.text: (4) The trustee may declare in writing a person to be incapacitated during any period in which the trustee is of the opinion that such person is unable freely to exercise his rights or powers or to fu...

636.text: (1) Subject to Article 85A(2), to the extent any person is granted the power under the terms of a trust to demand or request any act on the part of a settlor, beneficiary, trustee, protector, or other...

637.text: (a) to accept or recognise only demands or requests, or the effects of any approval, veto, or compelled action or the exercise of any power, which are given by or are the result of persons acting of t...

638.text: (b) to ignore any demands or requests, or the effects of any approval, veto, or compelled action or the exercise of any power, where the person attempting to demand, request, approve, veto, compel the...

639.text: (2) Article 85A(1) shall not apply to any decision, directive, notice or order issued by the Registrar in relation to a trust, pursuant to the powers of the Registrar under the Operating Law.

641.text: PART 11: PROVISIONS APPLICABLE TO A FOREIGN TRUST

643.text: (1) Subject to Article 86(2), a foreign trust shall be regarded as being governed by, and shall be interpreted in accordance with, its governing law.

644.text: (2) A foreign trust shall be unenforceable in the DIFC:

645.text: (a) to the extent that it purports:

646.text:
(i) to do anything which is contrary to DIFC law; or   
(ii) to confer any right or power or impose any obligation the exercise of which is contrary to DIFC law; or

647.text: (b) to the extent that the Court declares that the trust is immoral or contrary to public policy in the DIFC,

648.text: but otherwise shall be enforceable in accordance with its terms and its governing law.

653.text: (1) In the Law, a reference to:

654.text:
(a) a statutory provision includes a reference to the statutory provision as amended or re-enacted from time to time;   
(b) a “person” includes any natural person, body corporate or body unincorporat...

655.text: (2) The headings in the Law shall not affect its interpretation.

656.text: (3) A reference in this Law to a Part, Chapter, Article or Schedule by number only, and without further identification, is a reference to the Part, Chapter, Article or Schedule of that number in this ...

660.text: References to legislation and Guidance in the Law shall be construed in accordance with the following provisions:

663.text: In the Law, unless the context indicates otherwise, the defined terms listed below shall have the corresponding meanings:

664.text:
TermDefinition
Arbitration Lawthe DIFC Arbitration Law...

671.text: SCHEDULE 2 APPLICATION OF THE ARBITRATION LAW

672.text: 1. The Arbitration Law shall apply and be construed with respect to a trust arbitration, as stated hereunder.

673.text: 2. In the Arbitration Law, “dispute” includes an administration question.

674.text: 3. Article 12(1) of the Arbitration Law shall apply as if it read - “The settlor of a trust shall be free to determine (by provision in the trust instrument) how, in relation to a trust, disputes are ...

675.text: 4. Where in the Arbitration Law reference is made to a matter agreed between the parties to an arbitration agreement (including a matter which may be authorised, chosen, conferred, designated, nominat...

676.text: 5. Neither Article 12 of the Arbitration Law nor any rule of law or construction treating an arbitration agreement separate to any agreement of which it is a part shall apply in relation to a trust ar...

677.text: 6. The term “action” in Article 13 of the Arbitration Law includes an application or other reference to the Court concerning an administration question which the trust instrument requires to be submit...

678.text: 7. In any application or other reference to the Court referred to in paragraph 6, the Court may stay the proceedings on its own volition unless all parties in relation to the trust affected by the app...

679.text: 8. Article 38 of the Arbitration Law shall apply as if it included the following Articles:

680.text: “(6) Where a person is or has been a party to a trust arbitration in the capacity of trustee he shall, unless the tribunal otherwise orders, be entitled to the costs of the arbitration, in so far as t...

681.text: (7) Where a person is or has been a party to a trust arbitration in the capacity of trustee and is entitled to be paid his costs out of the fund held by the trustee any doubt as to whether costs were ...

682.text: 9. For the purposes of enforcing an arbitral award under Article 41(2)(a)(iii) of the Arbitration Law, the term “dispute” includes an administration question.
